Case Study

From a two-page brief
to a fully simulated
clinical workforce

A nine-stage business simulation with gamification that transformed an unstructured onboarding process into a rigorous, confidence-building eLearning programme for Clinical Research Associates.

Client Leading Contract Research Organisation
Sector Pharmaceutical / Clinical Research
Timeline Four months
At a glance
9stages
Workflow stages simulated end-to-end within a single programme
95%+
Post-assessment scores across all learners at rollout
2pg
Source brief — the entire programme was built from two pages of input
1tool
Articulate Storyline 360 — modular SCORM packages per stage
The challenge

New hires were entering the field
without a structured path to get there.

Clinical Research Associates carry significant responsibility. They are the organisation's eyes and ears inside hospitals — visiting trial sites, reviewing patient records, inspecting drug storage, and submitting detailed monitoring reports on behalf of pharmaceutical clients.

Yet when new CRAs joined, their onboarding was informal and inconsistent. There was no structured training programme, no defined learning path, and no mechanism to verify that each associate was truly ready before being deployed to a live clinical trial site.

The organisation needed a training intervention that could onboard new hires quickly and confidently — and serve as a meaningful refresher for experienced CRAs already in the field.

  • No source content existed beyond a two-page document listing nine workflow steps with brief descriptions
  • All knowledge resided with subject matter experts — no manuals, no reference guides, no existing training materials
  • The development team operated in a time zone different from the client, requiring disciplined collaboration and communication
  • The programme had to serve both new hires encountering the role for the first time and experienced CRAs seeking structured reinforcement
  • Clinical accuracy was non-negotiable — errors in the field carry direct regulatory and patient safety implications
The approach

A nine-stage business simulation — each stage mirroring a real step in the CRA monitoring workflow, with gamification woven through every level to sustain engagement and reward mastery.

01
Arranging the Visit
Scheduling and coordinating with hospital staff ahead of the monitoring visit
02
Visit Preparation
Activity prioritisation, documentation review, and pre-visit checklist completion
03
Arriving at the Hospital
Physically navigating the site and connecting with the right personnel on the day
04
Patient Records Review
ICF review and Source Data Verification (SDV/SDR) for all patients enrolled in the trial
05
eCRF Data Confirmation
Cross-checking that patient data has been accurately entered into the trial database
06
Meeting with Hospital Staff
Structured discussions with key clinical team members to address findings and queries
07
Pharmacy Inspection
Inspection of investigational drug supply, storage conditions, and accountability records
08
Regulatory Document Review
Audit of administrative and regulatory files, cross-checked against patient records
09
Write & Submit the Monitoring Report
The culminating stage — compiling findings from all eight preceding steps into a comprehensive, compliant monitoring visit report submitted to the organisation
Stage-based reward points
Learners earn points within each stage, creating a sense of progression and achievement at every step
Summative assessment gates
Progression to the next stage is unlocked only after clearing each level's assessment — ensuring mastery before advancement
Modular SCORM architecture
Each stage is a separate SCORM package — enabling flexible deployment, granular tracking, and targeted refresher access
The design insight
The problem we solved

Building a complete curriculum from two pages

When the project commenced, the client had no source material beyond a two-page document. There were no manuals, no reference guides, no existing training artefacts. Every piece of learning content had to be extracted through structured conversations with subject matter experts and shaped into a curriculum that could withstand regulatory scrutiny.

The turning point A working prototype was developed early — built from the two-page brief alone — to give the client a concrete sense of direction. The client responded immediately and positively, unlocking deeper collaboration and a steady flow of expert knowledge that fuelled the full programme build.
What made this work

Simulation designed around real workflow, not theory

Rather than teaching the monitoring process as abstract knowledge, the programme placed learners inside the workflow itself. Each of the nine stages recreated the decisions, documents, and interactions a CRA would encounter in the field — from scheduling hospital visits to reconciling patient data in the eCRF system.

The big-picture workflow was presented at the outset and revisited at regular intervals throughout the programme, reinforcing the mental model of the full process even as learners worked through individual stages in depth.

Programme in action
01
Programme overview & nine-stage workflow map
02
Stage entry — gamification reward interface
03
Hospital navigation simulation screen
04
Patient records review — interactive SDV activity
05
Pharmacy inspection scenario
06
Summative assessment & stage completion screen
The outcome

CRAs entered the field faster,
more prepared, and more confident.

The organisation was able to accelerate CRA hiring and deployment, with the structured programme removing the bottleneck of informal, inconsistent onboarding. New associates completed the programme at their own pace and arrived at trial sites with a clear, tested understanding of the full monitoring workflow.

Learners consistently reported that the simulation felt close to the real experience — a direct result of designing each stage around authentic field activities rather than abstract content.

"For the first time, our new CRAs arrive at sites already knowing the process. The simulation made it real for them before they'd even taken their first visit."
— Head of Clinical Operations
95%+
Post-assessment scores All learners scored 95% or above across the nine-stage programme

End-to-end workflow coverage Every step of the monitoring visit simulated within a single coherent programme
↑hire
Accelerated deployment The organisation ramped up CRA hiring and field deployment, directly improving capacity and revenue
100%
